purpose: Spending category taxonomy for the expense-analyzer skill. Used for categorizing transactions, benchmarking against typical budgets, and generating actionable insights.

categorization_rules.priority: Match by merchant name first, then by transaction description keyword, then by amount pattern (recurring = subscription candidate)
categorization_rules.unknown: Place uncategorized transactions in 'Other' but flag them for user review
categorization_rules.recurring_signals: Same merchant + similar amount within 25–35 days = likely subscription
categorization_rules.splitting: If a transaction clearly spans two categories (e.g. Amazon = shopping OR groceries OR electronics), use largest-probability category and note it

housing.label: Housing
housing.benchmark_insight: Housing should ideally be ≤30% of gross income. Above 40% indicates housing cost burden.

food_and_dining.label: Food & Dining
food_and_dining.benchmark_insight: Average US household spends ~10-15% of income on food. Dining out vs. groceries ratio reveals adherence opportunity.
food_and_dining.savings_signal: If food delivery > 25% of total food spend, that's typically a high-savings opportunity.

transportation.label: Transportation
transportation.benchmark_insight: Transportation above 20% of income is a red flag. Rideshare spend >$200/month often has substitution options.

health_and_medical.label: Health & Medical
health_and_medical.benchmark_insight: Out-of-pocket health costs vary widely. Gym membership unused for 2+ months is a common cancellation candidate.

shopping.label: Shopping
shopping.benchmark_insight: Shopping spikes often correlate with emotional spending events. Compare month-over-month to identify patterns.

entertainment.label: Entertainment
entertainment.benchmark_insight: Subscription stacking is common — list all active streaming/software subscriptions and review for overlap.

travel.label: Travel
travel.benchmark_insight: Travel is often lumpy (rare but large). Annualize by dividing 12-month total by 12 for true monthly budget picture.

education.label: Education
education.benchmark_insight: Student loan payments above 10% of take-home income signal potential refinancing opportunity.

financial.label: Financial
financial.benchmark_insight: Financial category isolates true discretionary spend from savings/debt service. Target 20% gross income to savings.

subscriptions.label: Subscriptions
subscriptions.benchmark_insight: List every subscription with last-used date. Unused or low-value subscriptions are the fastest savings win.
subscriptions.audit_prompt: Flag any subscription charged 3+ times with no obvious usage pattern — likely forgotten.

business.label: Business Expenses
business.benchmark_insight: Flag potential tax-deductible business expenses for the user to verify with their accountant.

other.label: Other / Uncategorized
other.benchmark_insight: A large 'Other' bucket means merchant matching needs improvement or user review is needed.

50_30_20.name: 50/30/20 Rule
50_30_20.description: 50% of take-home pay to needs (housing, food, transport, health), 30% to wants (dining out, entertainment, shopping), 20% to savings and debt payoff

zero_based.name: Zero-Based Budget
zero_based.description: Every dollar assigned a job. Income - all expenses + savings = $0. Best for aggressive debt payoff or savings goals.

pay_yourself_first.name: Pay Yourself First
pay_yourself_first.description: Automatically save/invest before anything else. Spend the remainder however needed. Best for high earners with variable spending.

savings_projection_rates.description: Use these multipliers to show how monthly savings changes compound. Assume 6% annual return (0.5%/month) for investment projections.
